text: Which of the following best defines opportunity cost?

options.A: It is the cost of producing those goods most desired by a given economy
options.B: It is the cost of the input mix that will lead to the greatest rate of growth for a given company
options.C: It is the amount of one product that must be given up in order to produce an additional unit of another product
options.D: It is the use of the least-cost method of production
options.E: It is the cost of labor used in the production process

text: Which of the following explains why a production possibilities curve is often represented as concave (bowed out) from the origin?

options.A: The law of demand
options.B: The law of supply
options.C: Constant returns to scale
options.D: Decreasing opportunity cost
options.E: Increasing opportunity cost

text: Assume that consumers consider popcorn and pretzels to be substitutes. A significant decrease in the supply of popcorn will affect the pretzel market by

options.A: increasing the demand for pretzels and therefore the supply of pretzels
options.B: increasing the demand for pretzels and therefore the price of pretzels
options.C: decreasing the demand for pretzels and therefore the price of pretzels
options.D: increasing the supply of pretzels and therefore the price of pretzels
options.E: decreasing the supply of pretzels and therefore the price of pretzels

text: In the market depicted in the diagram above, if the government imposes a price ceiling of $1.00 per gallon on gasoline, which of the following will result?

options.A: A surplus of 6 billion gallons
options.B: A shortage of 6 billion gallons
options.C: A surplus of 12 billion gallons
options.D: A shortage of 12 billion gallons
options.E: Neither a surplus nor a shortage, because the price ceiling would not be effective

text: If a 5 percent wage increase in a particular labor market results in a 10 percent decrease in employment, the demand for labor in that market is

options.A: double the purchase of goods X and Y
options.B: buy more of good X and less of good Y
options.C: buy less of good X and more of good Y
options.D: buy less of both goods X and Y
options.E: buy the same amounts of goods X and Y

text: If the average total cost of producing 5 units of a good is $10 and the average total cost of producing 6 units of the same good is $13, then the marginal cost of the 6th unit is

options.A: fall initially, but eventually rise
options.B: rise initially, but eventually fall
options.C: rise consistently due to diminishing return
options.D: rise consistently due to the advantages of specialization
options.E: rise consistently due to economies of scale

text: In the short run, which of the following costs must continuously decrease as output produced increases?

options.A: Total variable cost
options.B: Total fixed cost
options.C: Average variable cost
options.D: Average fixed cost
options.E: Average total cost Questions 8-9 refer to the graph below

text: If a firm is maximizing its economic profits and the marginal product of the last unit of labor hired is 10 units per day, the wage rate is $100 per day, and the firm's output sells for $20 per unit, at what output level is the firm producing?
Which of the following is most likely to occur if the firm increases production beyond 10 units?

options.A: Consumers would be willing to purchase more than 10 units at the price of $20 per unit
options.B: The firm would definitely experience a loss
options.C: The firm would have to lower its price to sell more than 10 units
options.D: The firm’s average cost of production would initially increase
options.E: The firm’s profits would increase

text: Which of the following statements about a constant-cost perfectly competitive industry in long-run equilibrium must be true?

options.A: An increase in demand will cause no change in the long-run equilibrium price
options.B: An increase in demand will cause no change in the long-run equilibrium quantity
options.C: The long-run supply curve is upward sloping
options.D: The long-run supply curve is perfectly inelastic
options.E: The total cost of production remains the same as output increases

text: In which of the following market structures do firms recognize their mutual interdependence?

options.A: Oligopoly
options.B: Monopoly
options.C: Perfect competition
options.D: Unregulated natural monopoly
options.E: Monopsony

text: Assume that a profit-maximizing firm is perfectly competitive in both the output and the factor markets and is at its long-run equilibrium. The firm’s output is 100 units, its total revenue is $600.00, and the fixed cost of production is $50.00. Based on this information, which of the following is true for the firm?

options.A: Its marginal cost is $5.50, and its average total cost is $5.50
options.B: Its marginal cost is $5.50, and its average variable cost is $5.50
options.C: Its marginal cost is $6.00, and its average total cost is $5.50
options.D: Its marginal cost is $6.00, and its average fixed cost is $5.50
options.E: Its marginal cost is $6.00, and its average variable cost is $5.50

text: Businesses employ workers from city neighborhoods and rural areas. These workers are perfect substitutes and cannot relocate in the short run. The government offers businesses a wage subsidy if they hire workers from city neighborhoods. What is the effect of the subsidy on the wage rate of rural workers and on the total hours they work?

options.A: Increase Decrease
options.B: No change Increase
options.C: No change Decrease
options.D: Decrease Decrease
options.E: Decrease No change

headers: Total Hours Worked by Rural Workers

text: A per-unit tax on pollution produced by a firm will affect the firm's output and pollution levels in which of the following ways?

options.A: Increase Increase
options.B: Increase Decrease
options.C: Decrease Increase
options.D: Decrease Decrease
options.E: No change No change

text: Which of the following will most likely lead to a more equal distribution of income?

options.A: More regressive national sales tax
options.B: More progressive income taxes
options.C: An increase in the high school dropout rate
options.D: An increase in structural unemployment
options.E: An increase in earnings for owners of capital

text: Assume both Italy and Greece produce only two goods: wine and olive oil. If Italy holds a comparative advantage in the production of wine, then which of the following statements is NOT true?

options.A: Italy must hold an absolute advantage in the production of wine
options.B: Greece holds a comparative advantage in the production of olive oil
options.C: Italy’s opportunity cost of producing one additional unit of wine is lower than Greece’s
options.D: Greece’s opportunity cost of producing one additional unit of olive oil is lower than Italy’s
options.E: If trade is open between them, these countries have an incentive to trade

text: Following a prolonged power outage, the price of flashlights normally increases significantly. If city officials systematically impose a price ceiling on flashlights during such emergencies, in the long run the number of flashlights available is likely to

options.A: make flashlights more available
options.B: create a shortage of flashlights
options.C: shift the demand curve for flashlights to the right
options.D: shift the supply curve for flashlights to the left
options.E: have no effect on the availability of flashlights  Questions 18-19 are based on the diagram below, which shows the effect of a unit tax placed on a good

text: What is the price paid by consumers and the net price received by producers after the tax is paid?
According to the diagram, what is the dollar amount of the unit tax?
According to the law of diminishing marginal utility, which of the following is true?

options.A: Total satisfaction decreases as more units of a good are consumed
options.B: The additional satisfaction received from consuming extra units of a good decreases as consumption of the good increases
options.C: The additional satisfaction received from consuming extra units of a good decreases as consumption of the good decreases
options.D: The additional satisfaction received from consuming extra units of a good increases as consumption of the good increases
options.E: When marginal utility is decreasing, total utility is decreasing

text: Within the range of market demand, which of the following is consistent with the conditions of a natural monopoly?

options.A: Long-run total cost decreases as output increases
options.B: Long-run average total cost remains constant as output increases
options.C: Long-run average total cost decreases as output increases
options.D: Marginal cost exceeds average cost
options.E: Setting price equal to marginal cost will maximize profits

text: The graph above shows the long-run average total cost curve for a firm. In the long run, if the firm increases its output from Q1 to Q2, its average total cost

options.A: upward sloping
options.B: downward sloping
options.C: horizontal
options.D: vertical
options.E: U-shaped

text: A constant-cost, perfectly competitive gadget industry is in long-run equilibrium. An increase in the number of consumers of gadgets will most likely result in

options.A: higher short-run and long-run prices for gadgets
options.B: reduced short-run profits, followed by the exit of some firms
options.C: an upward shift in all short-run cost curves, followed by a higher long-run price for gadgets
options.D: a higher short-run price for gadgets, followed by an increase in the quantity produced
options.E: a decrease in the demand for gadgets, followed by a decrease in the supply of gadgets

text: The profit-maximizing firm depicted in the graph above should

options.A: exit if conditions do not improve in the long run
options.B: produce the output that minimizes average total cost
options.C: increase price to maximize profits
options.D: increase output to maximize profits
options.E: use less capital and more labor to reduce cost

text: If the average variable cost of producing 5 units of a good is $100 and the average variable cost of producing 6 units of the same good is $100, the marginal cost of increasing output from 5 to 6 units is

options.E: $500  Questions 26-27  are based on the following table, which shows the relationship between the number of workers and coal output (in tons per day). Number of Workers Total Output of Coal 0 0 1 25 2 44 3 60 4 70 5 75

text: How many workers would the coal company want to hire if the price of coal were competitively priced at $5 per ton and the wage rate were $40 per day?
The marginal physical product of the second worker is
If a firm's total revenue is $150,000 and its explicit costs are $50,000, its accounting profit is
The government can increase allocative efficiency by

options.A: taxing the producer of the good
options.B: subsidizing the producer of the good
options.C: prosecuting firms that produce the good without proper permits
options.D: setting a price ceiling to encourage production of the good
options.E: setting a price floor to discourage production of the good

text: Which of the following is the best example of a pure public good?

options.A: Electricity from a public utility
options.B: Mail delivery service by the post office
options.C: Social Security payments
options.D: National defense
options.E: Imported oil

text: According to the information in the table above, which of the following statements is true if both countries have the same number of workers?

options.A: Country A has both an absolute and a comparative advantage in manufactured goods
options.B: Country A has an absolute advantage in manufactured goods but a comparative advantage in service goods
options.C: Country B has a comparative advantage in service goods but no absolute advantage in either good
options.D: Country A has an absolute advantage in service goods but a comparative advantage in manufactured goods
options.E: Country B has an absolute advantage in manufactured goods, but without more information, it is not possible to tell in which product it has a comparative advantage

text: Which of the following would most likely result in a decrease in the equilibrium price of oranges?

options.A: The weather during this orange-growing season is not as good as it was last year
options.B: The price of apples, a substitute for oranges, is higher this year than last year
options.C: New studies suggest that oranges contain traces of cancer-causing substances due to pesticide residue
options.D: A tree-killing fungus spreads through orange orchards across the country
options.E: Firms in the orange industry launch an effective advertising campaign

text: If a severe drought destroys a significant portion of the peanut crop and peanut farmers’ revenues increase, which of the following is true over the observed range of prices?

options.A: The demand for peanuts must be unit price elastic
options.B: The demand for peanuts must be price elastic
options.C: The demand for peanuts must be price inelastic
options.D: The supply of peanuts must be price inelastic
options.E: The supply of peanuts must be price elastic

text: Promoters of a rock group know that if they charged $8 a ticket, 400 people would buy tickets for a concert. If they charged $7 a ticket instead, 500 people would buy tickets. Over this price range, the demand for concert tickets for the rock group is

options.A: elastic
options.B: inelastic
options.C: unit elastic
options.D: perfectly elastic
options.E: perfectly inelastic

text: If the price of onions increases, the demand for onions will most likely

options.A: remain unchanged because hamburgers and onions are different goods
options.B: increase because hamburgers and onions are substitutes
options.C: increase because hamburgers and onions are complements
options.D: decrease because hamburgers and onions are complements
options.E: decrease because hamburgers and onions are substitutes

text: If labor is the only variable input in the production process, the short-run marginal cost curve is upward sloping because which of the following occurs as more and more labor is added?

options.A: Output decreases, and thus marginal cost increases
options.B: Output increases, and thus marginal cost increases
options.C: Output increases at an increasing rate, and thus the cost of producing each additional unit of output increases
options.D: Output increases at a decreasing rate, and thus the cost of producing each additional unit of output increases
options.E: Output increases at a decreasing rate, and thus the cost of producing each additional unit of output decreases

text: At a firm’s current rate of output, the marginal cost is $65, the average variable cost is $35, the average fixed cost is $30, and the product price is $65. Which of the following statements is true for the firm?

options.A: Economic profits are zero because marginal revenue equals marginal cost
options.B: Economic profits are negative because total revenue is less than total cost
options.C: Economic profits are positive because total revenue is greater than total cost
options.D: Economic profits are negative because price is greater than average variable cost
options.E: Economic profits are zero because price equals average total cost

text: The graph above shows the short-run cost curves of a firm in a perfectly competitive market. Which of the following are true at the firm’s profit- maximizing output level? I. Price exceeds average total cost. II. Economic profits are zero. III. Marginal cost equals average total cost. IV. New firms are likely to enter the market in the long run.

options.A: I and II only
options.B: I and III only
options.C: I and IV only
options.D: II, III, and IV only
options.E: I, II, III, and IV

text: Which of the following best describes an oligopolistic market?

options.A: Many sellers with identical products and no barriers to entry
options.B: Many sellers, each with a clearly differentiated product, and no barriers to entry
options.C: A few competing sellers with similar products and high barriers to entry
options.D: A few competing sellers of identical products and no barriers to entry
options.E: No competition among sellers and high barriers to entry

text: The payoff matrix below shows the per-unit profits associated with the production strategies of two utility companies, UA and UB. Each firm has two choices: to reduce production by 10 percent or by 20 percent. The first entry in each cell indicates the profits to UA, and the second, the profits to UB. UB Reduces Production by 20 Percent Reduces Production by 10 Percent UA Reduces Production by 20 Percent $150, $150 $50, $250 Reduces Production by 10 Percent $250, $50 $100, $100 Based on the information, and assuming no cooperation, which of the following statements is true?

options.A: Neither company has a dominant strategy
options.B: Both companies have an incentive to reduce production by 10%
options.C: Both companies have an incentive to reduce production by 20%
options.D: Only UA has an incentive to reduce production by 20%
options.E: Only UB has an incentive to reduce production by 20%

text: In order to minimize short-run losses, a profit- maximizing firm will necessarily shut down production under which of the following conditions?

options.A: Total revenue is less than total cost
options.B: Marginal cost is greater than average total cost
options.C: Marginal cost is less than marginal revenue
options.D: Average revenue is less than average variable cost
options.E: Average revenue is less than average total cost

text: The graph above shows the short-run cost curves of a firm in a perfectly competitive market. Which of the following are true at the firm's profit-maximizing output level?

options.A: a derived demand
options.B: decreasing
options.C: relatively elastic
options.D: relatively inelastic
options.E: unit elastic

text: If a firm employs only labor and capital in its production process, which of the following best describes the optimal combination of inputs for the firm in the long run?

options.A: The marginal product per dollar spent on labor is equal to the marginal product per dollar spent on capital
options.B: The marginal product of labor is equal to the marginal product of capital
options.C: The total product of labor is equal to the total product of capital
options.D: The marginal product of labor and capital are both zero
options.E: All marginal products are equal to all average products

text: Most economists argue that a monopoly is inefficient because it

options.A: has no incentive to minimize its costs
options.B: produces too little output and sets a price above marginal cost
options.C: earns too much profit by charging consumers any price it wants
options.D: produces too much output and thus wastes scarce resources
options.E: usually produces unsafe products if not regulated by government

text: The table above shows the distribution of income in Country X in 2010 before and after taxes and transfer payments. Which of the following can be concluded about the effect of the government’s tax and transfer policies on income distribution in Country X?

options.A: The distribution of income is significantly less equal after accounting for the impact of government policies
options.B: The distribution of income is about the same after accounting for the impact of government policies
options.C: The largest gainers from government policies are the third and fourth quintile groups
options.D: The only quintile to benefit from government policies is the lowest quintile
options.E: The major transfer of income is from the highest quintile to the two lowest quintiles

text: The opportunity cost of attending the community college is
In which of the following situations is a good NOT scarce?

options.A: Consumers give up nothing to obtain more of the good
options.B: Consumers can purchase as much of the good as they want at its current market price
options.C: Large quantities of the good are available in the marketplace
options.D: There is a surplus of the good at some positive price
options.E: There is a shortage of the good at some positive price

text: Suppose that the market supply curve for shoes is upward sloping and the market demand curve is downward sloping. How will the imposition of a sales tax on shoes affect the consumer surplus, the producer surplus, and the total surplus?

options.A: Decrease Decrease Decrease
options.B: Decrease Increase Increase
options.C: Decrease Increase Decrease
options.D: Increase Decrease Decrease
options.E: Increase Increase Increase

text: A consumer is willing to pay $2,000 for a diamond ring but is able to purchase the ring for $1,500. The consumer's willingness to pay $2,000 for a diamond ring indicates that the

options.A: total utility of diamond rings is greater than the total utility of water
options.B: total utility of water is less than the marginal utility of a diamond ring
options.C: marginal utility of a bottle of water is less than the marginal utility of a diamond ring
options.D: marginal utility of a bottle of water is greater than the marginal utility of a diamond ring
options.E: consumer is irrational and does not understand that water is more important than a diamond

text: If a firm’s production process exhibits economies of scale, which of the following will occur when the firm’s output increases?

options.A: Its short-run average total costs will rise
options.B: Its long-run average total costs will rise
options.C: Its long-run average total costs will fall
options.D: Its short-run total costs will fall
options.E: Its long-run total costs will fall

text: Which of the following helps explain why the demand curve for a normal good is downward sloping?

options.A: The income and substitution effects move the quantity demanded in the same direction
options.B: The income effect moves the quantity demanded in the opposite direction of the substitution effect
options.C: The income effect dominates the substitution effect
options.D: The substitution effect dominates the income effect
options.E: With an increase in income, the consumer decreases consumption of the good

text: The graph above shows the total revenue and total cost curves for a firm in which type of market structure and what is the profit-maximizing quantity?

options.A: Monopoly Q2
options.B: Monopoly Q3
options.C: Perfect Competition Q1
options.D: Perfect Competition Q3
options.E: Perfect Competition Q4

text: For a monopoly, the marginal revenue curve is always below the demand curve because

options.A: equal to average revenue
options.B: less than average revenue
options.C: increasing
options.D: decreasing
options.E: greater than zero

text: Let P = price, MR = marginal revenue, MC = marginal cost, and ATC = average total cost. In monopolistic competition, which of the following most accurately describes the long-run equilibrium conditions for a firm?

options.A: P > ATC, MR = MC, and P > MC
options.B: P > ATC, MR > MC, and P = MC
options.C: P = ATC, MR = MC, and P > MC
options.D: P = ATC, MR = MC, and P = MC
options.E: P = ATC, MR > MC, and P > MC

text: In a perfectly competitive free market economy, a wage gap between two workers can be explained by differences in all of the following EXCEPT their

options.A: years of schooling
options.B: occupations
options.C: marginal products
options.D: marginal revenue products
options.E: tastes for luxury goods

text: Firm XYZ produces and sells corn in a perfectly competitive market and hires its workers in a perfectly competitive labor market. Which of the following best describes the demand curve for XYZ's corn and XYZ's demand curve for labor?

options.A: Horizontal Horizontal
options.B: Horizontal Downward sloping
options.C: Horizontal Vertical
options.D: Downward sloping Downward sloping
options.E: Downward sloping Horizontal

text: A perfectly competitive firm advertises in order to

options.A: shift the demand curve for its product to the left
options.B: make the demand for its product less price elastic
options.C: make its product more similar to its competitors’
options.D: increase its positive externalities
options.E: reduce the industry’s barriers to entry

text: A profit-maximizing firm will hire an input up to the point at which

options.A: marginal physical product equals marginal revenue product
options.B: marginal revenue equals marginal factor cost
options.C: marginal revenue product equals marginal revenue
options.D: marginal revenue product equals marginal factor cost
options.E: marginal physical product equals marginal factor cost Questions 59-60 refer to the diagram below, which shows a market’s cost and revenue curves

text: Which of the following statements correctly describes the relationship between the marginal social benefit and marginal social cost of providing the quantity of a public good at the socially optimal quantity?

options.A: Q1 P4 – P2
options.B: Q2 P3 – P2
options.C: Q3 P4 – P2
options.D: Q2 P3 – P1
options.E: Q3 P3 – P2

text: Given the information about the firm's long-run average total cost curve, one can conclude that the

options.A: private cost of producing good X exceeds the social cost of production at all levels of output
options.B: production of good X creates a negative externality
options.C: production of good X occurs in an imperfectly competitive market
options.D: market quantity, Q3, is the socially optimal quantity
